summary: Pachacamac temple ledger — the administrative register of one of the most powerful Andean oracles. 226 cords, 9 colors, 4 depth levels. Cord 1 alone carries 96 subsidiaries (a full hierarchical register of 41 entities). The body (cords 4-96) pairs numbers with text labels: 100=years, 31=fathers, 54=fathers(title), 57=paths. Unique 'spiral knots' noted by Urton — possibly a Pachacamac authentication mark. Coverage 95.7%.
interpretation:
IDENTIFICATION
  226 cords: 106 INT (sum=5156), 116 STRING, 4 EMPTY.
  6 clusters, 4 depth levels (L1-L4), paired architecture.
  Ethnologisches Museum, Berlin. Provenance: Pachacamac.
  Urton notes: 'unusual spiral knots with and without axis.'
  Coverage: 95.7% (111/116 STRING cords confirmed).
  9 colors: W(83), AB(58), BG(21), MB(20), LC(19), KB(11), VR(5), YB(4), GG(2).

TWO-SCALE STRUCTURE

  SCALE 1 — THE MEGA-TREE (cord 1)
    A single L1 cord (INT=41, white) carries 96 subsidiaries across
    4 levels. This is a complete hierarchical register in itself:
    41 entities (persons? families? plots?) detailed in a tree.
    Colors encode categories:
      W (white) = neutral data / amounts
      AB (subject) = named persons
      BG (identity) = identifiers
      LC (ritual) = sacred elements
      MB (category) = classifications
      KB (data) = subtotals
      VR (vermilion), YB (yellow-brown), GG (justice) = specialized
    Vocabulary in the tree: wapa (administrator), pata (terrain),
    papa (father), chana (canal), kaka (maternal uncle), waka (huaca),
    pama (formula), wayta (flower), paka (secret), taka (to stake).
    The full cadastral and ritual lexicon.

  SCALE 2 — THE PAIRED REGISTER (cords 4-96)
    Number-text pairs alternating:
      100 <-> wata (years)
      31 <-> papa (fathers, kinship)
      54 <-> tata (fathers, title)
      110 <-> GEN-wa (of me)
      37 <-> chay (this)
      57 <-> way (paths)
    A double-entry LEDGER: each number has its text label.
    Total INT value = 5,156 units across 106 numeric cords.

SECTIONS

  CLUSTER 1 (cord 1): The mega-tree. INT=41 with 96 subsidiaries.
    54 INT (sum=2598) + 42 STRING. The detailed breakdown.

  CLUSTER 2 (cords 2-3): Two empty cords. Separator.

  CLUSTER 3 (cords 4-56, 53 cords): The main body.
    17 INT (sum=1046) + 36 STRING.
    Opens with 1OBJ-pipa ('to me, who?') and maki ('hand/labor').
    Key terms: wata (year), tata (father), papa (father), pata
    (terrain), wapa (administrator), taka (to stake), pama (formula),
    waka (huaca x2), sina (like/similar), tina (spindle).
    Mixes accounting and ritual — the oracle's accounts.
    Notable: cord 52 wakama (huaca+DIR = toward the huaca),
    cord 30 pama-PASS (the formula, passively received).

  CLUSTER 4 (cords 57-96, 40 cords): The second ledger.
    23 INT (sum=851) + 16 STRING + 1 EMPTY.
    Opens with papa (father), chapa (guardian), taki-ACC (ritual[obj]),
    PASS-chaka (bridge[passive]), ACC-pata chay (the terrain, this).
    Ends with a long sequence of pure numbers (cords 86-96).
    The closing accounts.
    Notable: chaka (bridge) x2 — Pachacamac was connected by
    important bridges; the bridge is central to the site's architecture.

  CLUSTER 5 (cords 97-98): Closing signature.
    1OBJ-ku ('myself', reflexive) + INT=38.
    The administrator's personal mark.

  CLUSTER 6 (cord 99): Empty. End marker.

KEY VOCABULARY
  pata (terrain): x9 — the land being counted
  wapa (administrator): x8 — the managing authority
  papa (father, kinship): x6 — lineage holders
  tata (father, title): x4 — title-holders
  pama (formula): x4 — ritual formulas (oracle context)
  wata (year): x4 — temporal accounting
  pi (who?): x4 — interrogatives (queries in the register)
  way (path): x3 — routes/access
  chaka (bridge): x3 — Pachacamac's bridges
  chay (this/that): x3 — deictic references
  taka (to stake): x2 — boundary marking
  waka (huaca): x2 — the sacred site itself
  chama (food/strength): x2 — provisions/tribute

COLOR SYSTEM (9 colors)
  The most chromatically complex khipu analyzed so far.
  Colors function as column headers in a spreadsheet:
    W = amounts (neutral data)
    AB = subjects (persons involved)
    BG = identity (identifiers, names)
    LC = ritual (sacred elements, formulas)
    MB = category (classifications, subtotals)
    KB = data (secondary amounts)
    VR = vermilion (special values, L3/L4 deep entries)
    YB = yellow-brown (temporal markers?)
    GG = justice (appears only twice — cord 1 tree)

SPIRAL KNOTS
  Urton documents 'spiral knots with and without axis' found only
  on this khipu. These may be a Pachacamac authentication mark —
  a physical signature specific to the temple's administration,
  like a watermark or seal. No other khipu in the OKR has this
  knot type.

COMPARISON
  UR193 (Pachacamac ledger) vs HP020 (Pachacamac treasure map):
    Both from Pachacamac. UR193 is the temple's ACCOUNTS;
    HP020 describes the temple's GEOGRAPHY (cadastral survey).
    Together they document the economic and spatial administration
    of the Pachacamac oracle.
  UR193 vs UR050 (white land registry):
    UR050 is a simple linear cadastre (all white, 6 roots).
    UR193 is a complex hierarchical ledger (9 colors, 4 levels).
    UR050 = a village register. UR193 = a temple's grand livre.

NARRATIVE — THE GRAND LIVRE OF PACHACAMAC

  Pachacamac was the greatest oracle of the Andes — a vast temple
  complex on the coast south of Lima where pilgrims came from across
  the empire to hear the voice of the god. The temple needed
  administrators, accounts, records. This khipu is one of those
  records.

  It begins with a single cord, white, carrying the number 41. From
  this cord hang ninety-six subsidiaries in four levels of depth,
  colored in nine different hues. It is a tree — a complete register
  of forty-one entities, each broken down into sub-accounts by
  category. White cords carry the amounts. Blue-green cords mark
  identities. Lilac cords signal the sacred. The colors are the
  column headers of a spreadsheet that was never flat — it was
  three-dimensional, hanging in space.

  In this tree: the administrator (wapa), the terrain (pata), the
  father (papa), the canal (chana), the maternal uncle (kaka), the
  huaca (waka), the ritual formula (pama), the flower (wayta), the
  secret (paka). All the words of land and power and ritual, each
  attached to a number, each hanging from the branch above.

  After the tree, the ledger proper. Fifty-three cords pairing
  numbers with labels: one hundred years, thirty-one fathers,
  fifty-four title-holders, fifty-seven paths. The hand and labor
  (maki). The guardian (chapa). The ritual chant (taki). The bridge
  (chaka) — those bridges that connected Pachacamac's pyramids
  across the river valley, and that connected the living world to
  the oracle's voice.

  Then the closing accounts: a sequence of numbers growing sparse,
  the text labels thinning out, until only figures remain. The
  accountant tallies the last entries.

  The khipu ends with two cords: 'myself' and thirty-eight. The
  administrator signs the ledger. Thirty-eight what? We do not know.
  But he counted, and he signed.

  The knots on this khipu are unique. Urton found spiral knots —
  with and without axis — that appear nowhere else in the corpus.
  They may be the watermark of Pachacamac: the physical proof that
  this document was made at the temple, by the temple's own
  khipukamayuq. A seal that cannot be forged, because no one else
  ties knots in spirals.
references: Sivan 2026, ALBA Project — Locke Word Splitting & paired register analysis
Pachacamac temple complex: Eeckhout 2004, Pachacamac et l'Empire Inca
Urton spiral knot documentation: Urton 2003, Signs of the Inka Khipu
Color coding in khipus: Hyland 2014, The Chankas and the Priest
Compare HP020 (Pachacamac cadastral survey, same provenance)
Compare UR050 (linear white land registry — simple vs complex ledger)
